findings.title: Raw 10-seed per-run costs (basis of mean±std and t-tests) not saved in repo
findings.claim: The only persisted statistical artefact is the per-algorithm summary mean±std; the underlying 10 per-seed mean costs (computed transiently in benchmarks_run.py:82-92) are never written to disk.
findings.concern: Without the per-seed values a reviewer cannot re-run the paper's significance tests on the real data, which is exactly what enables the synthetic-resampling shortcut in finding ttest-on-synthetic-data.
findings.resolution: Authors: please commit the raw per-seed final-cost arrays (10 values per algorithm per task) so the reported t-tests can be reproduced from real data.
findings.reason: CSV fetched directly: header and DPO/TRPO rows match the quoted values exactly; benchmarks_run.py computes avg_final_vals per seed but df.to_csv at line 106 only writes the summary mean±std, confirming per-seed values are never persisted.

findings.title: Trained models for reproduction hosted on an external Dropbox link, not in repo
findings.quote: Download pre-trained models from a Dropbox link (due to size constraints); run python benchmarks_run.py to reproduce results.
findings.claim: Reproducing Table 1/Figure 1 from pretrained weights requires downloading model files from an external Dropbox URL referenced in the README; the weights are not version-controlled in the repo (benchmarks/models/ is gitignored).
findings.concern: Off-repo, unversioned hosting (Dropbox) is a durability/reproducibility risk if the link rots, and the served weights are not tied to a commit; however dfPO models are tiny (0.17 MB, Table 6) and retrainable, so impact is limited.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm the Dropbox link is permanent (or mirror the weights / a Zenodo DOI), and state which commit produced them. (Quote is a paraphrased README summary; verify exact wording on the repo.)
findings.reason: README fetched and confirms the repo requires downloading two model folders (models/ and benchmarks/models/) from a Dropbox link before running benchmarks; quote is a faithful paraphrase as auditor noted.

findings.title: requirements.txt unpinned except pyrosetta — environment not reconstructable
findings.quote:
gym
gymnasium
numpy
pyrosetta==2023.12+release.1d87148
sb3_contrib
scipy
stable_baselines3
torch
findings.claim: Only pyrosetta carries a version pin; numpy, torch, stable_baselines3, sb3_contrib, scipy, gym/gymnasium and the rest are unpinned.
findings.concern: Baselines are built on Stable-Baselines3 whose default hyperparameters and RNG behavior change across versions, so the exact reported baseline numbers cannot be guaranteed to rebuild; also pyrosetta is a non-free dependency that may block re-running the molecular-dynamics task.
findings.resolution: Authors: pin exact versions (or ship a lockfile / environment.yml) for at least numpy, torch, stable_baselines3, sb3_contrib, and gymnasium. (Quote condenses requirements.txt; verify the full file.)
findings.reason: requirements.txt fetched: 14 packages listed (gym, gymnasium, imageio, matplotlib, numpy, opencv_python, pandas, pyrosetta==2023.12+release.1d87148, sb3_contrib, scipy, Shapely, stable_baselines3, torch, torchdiffeq); pyrosetta is the only pinned entry, confirming the core claim; quote is a condensed subset as noted.

findings.title: Paper says t-tests on seed-level means; code tests synthetic resamples
findings.quote: Statistical comparisons are conducted using t-tests on the seed-level means.
findings.claim: The paper (Appendix C.2) describes the significance tests as t-tests on the 10 seed-level means; the repo's analysis.ipynb instead runs t-tests on np.random.normal draws synthesized from the reported summary mean/std (see methodology finding ttest-on-synthetic-data).
findings.concern: The described procedure (test on real seed means) and the implemented procedure (test on fabricated Gaussian samples) disagree; this is the paper-vs-code facing of the same defect whose primary owner is the methodology finding.
findings.resolution: Authors: either run the t-test on the actual per-seed means, or correct the Appendix text; clarify which was intended.
findings.reason: paper_text.txt line 1434 contains the exact quoted sentence 'Statistical comparisons are conducted using t-tests on the seed-level means'; analysis.ipynb confirmed to use np.random.normal synthetic draws instead.

findings.title: No commit/tag pins the camera-ready paper to a repo state
findings.claim: The repo has 0 release tags and a moving master branch; the README does not state which commit corresponds to the NeurIPS 2025 paper. Latest commit 9dc65e7 (2025-10-23) post-dates the submission cycle.
findings.concern: Audits and reproductions can drift against a moving branch; there is no immutable snapshot tied to the published numbers.
findings.resolution: Authors: tag the commit that produced the paper's tables/figures. (Quote condenses the gh-api commit listing; verify on the repo.)
findings.reason: gh api confirms 0 releases and the tags endpoint returned 404 (no tags); latest commit is 9dc65e7 dated 2025-10-23, confirming the quoted commit SHA and date; no tag ties the paper to a repo state.

findings.title: Reported significance t-tests run on synthetic Gaussian draws, not experimental data
findings.quote:
np.random.seed(42)
n = 10
dpo_md = np.random.normal(loc=6.296, scale=0.048, size=n)
crossq_md = np.random.normal(loc=6.365, scale=0.030, size=n)
stat_md, pval_md = ttest_ind(dpo_md, crossq_md, equal_var=False)
findings.claim: analysis.ipynb cell 4 hard-codes each algorithm's reported summary mean and std, draws n=10 fresh samples per group via np.random.normal(loc=mean, scale=std), and runs scipy ttest_ind on those synthetic samples to produce the paper's significance verdicts; the real per-seed means (computed in benchmarks_run.py:82-92) are never used.
findings.concern: The p-values are a function of the (mean,std) the authors already report plus a fixed RNG seed, not of the experimental observations, so they cannot evidence statistical significance of dfPO over baselines and the Appendix C.2 significance claims are unsupported by the data.
findings.resolution: Authors: recompute all t-tests directly on the 10 actual per-seed mean costs (Welch / paired as appropriate), report the test used and one- vs two-sided, and update Table 4 / Appendix C.2 verdicts accordingly.
findings.paper_ref: Appendix C.2, Table 4; NeurIPS checklist Q7
findings.reason: analysis.ipynb fetched: cell contains np.random.seed(42), n=10, and np.random.normal(loc=..., scale=...) draws for each algorithm followed by ttest_ind calls, matching the quoted code exactly; per-seed means from benchmarks_run.py are not loaded or used anywhere in the notebook.

findings.title: Sub-0.001 across-seed std for several baselines questions 10-seed independence
findings.quote:
dpo_mol = np.random.normal(loc=53.352, scale=0.055, size=n)
ddpg_mol = np.random.normal(loc=68.203, scale=0.001, size=n)
stat_mol, pval_mol = ttest_ind(dpo_mol, ddpg_mol, equal_var=False)
findings.claim: For molecular dynamics the test draws DDPG samples with std=0.001 (the reported summary std), yielding the reproduced t=-948.9, p=8.0e-24 (see _audit_code/out/ttest_synthetic.csv); such near-zero across-seed variance for an off-policy baseline implies near-deterministic behavior across 10 seeds.
findings.concern: Setting aside the synthetic-data problem (owner: ttest-on-synthetic-data), the sub-0.001 reported across-seed std for several baselines (DDPG, TQC, S-DDPG in Table 4) is suspiciously small and may indicate the 10 seeds did not actually vary the baseline outcome (e.g. shared cached rollout).
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm the 10 baseline seeds produced genuinely independent runs and report the real per-seed spread.
findings.reason: analysis.ipynb confirms the quoted code with ddpg_mol using scale=0.001; CSV confirms DDPG molecular dynamics std rounds to 0.000, TQC to 0.001, S-DDPG to 0.001 — near-zero variance is present in the source data, making the concern valid.
